title: Asukas Roman Bath
wikitext:
[]: Asuka's "Roman Bath"? Post by fadingreminder » Wed Oct 10, 2007 10:45 am Alright, I understand how a big theory is her possible suicide attempt in some undisclosed tub, but where did the term "Roman bath" come from? I saw it in the Evapaedia and it just confused me as to where the term originated.

[]: Re: Asuka's "Roman Bath"? Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Oct 10, 2007 10:57 am fadingreminder wrote: Alright, I understand how a big theory is her possible suicide attempt in some undisclosed tub, but where did the term "Roman bath" come from? I saw it in the Evapaedia and it just confused me as to where the term originated.

[]: Post by fadingreminder » Wed Oct 10, 2007 11:00 am Makes sense I suppose... I've always heard of bloodletting yourself to death in a warm tub, I never knew the origin though. It's a new term to me anyway. Thanks!

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Oct 10, 2007 2:22 pm IIRC, I introduced the concept into this particular circle, by alluding to Asuka's actions as "taking the traditional Roman way out", as taken by luminaries such as Lucan, or the transitory Emperor, Quintillus, amongst others. It got collapsed to "Roman Bath" here by habit of usage. Any more should probably go in http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=3093
